profile.name: James
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner and day-to-day financial organizer; tends to track accounts and statements.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High (comfortable with investment accounts, taxes, and portfolio concepts; tends to reference specific balances and categories).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but methodical; prefers diversified, evidence-based choices and gets uneasy with big, unplanned swings or unclear liquidity—will ask how alternatives and taxable holdings behave in down markets.
profile.communication_style: Direct and structured; uses specific numbers, asks clarifying questions, and will correct small inaccuracies. Speaks in complete thoughts but still uses light hedging like "roughly" or "if I’m remembering right" when recalling dates or labels.
emotional_tendencies: mild anxiety around taxes and surprises
calm when there is a plan and timeline
profile.decision_making_style: Research-and-verify; wants a clear rationale, trade-offs, and a written summary before agreeing.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting; open to guidance but expects transparency on fees, assumptions, and implementation steps.
internal_biases: anchoring to current account balances and recent performance
overconfidence in ability to optimize details
hidden_concerns: whether their income sources are reliable enough to support long-term income goals
whether estate planning objectives are being addressed beyond just investments

profile.name: Mary
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; focuses on big-picture lifestyle goals and timing, less involved in account-by-account tracking.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate (understands budgeting and retirement goals; less fluent in account types and tax nuances, may mix up terms like gross vs net or balance vs payment).
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate with a preference for stability; comfortable investing when it’s framed around dependable income and protection, but may push back if the plan feels too complex or illiquid.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and story-driven; speaks in round numbers ("about," "around") and occasionally says "I might be off" when recalling specifics. Will interrupt to clarify what something means in plain English and how it affects monthly cash flow.
emotional_tendencies: stress when discussions get overly technical
relief when there’s a simple, repeatable process
profile.decision_making_style: Values-based and collaborative; wants to align decisions with lifestyle and family/legacy intentions, then relies on James for numeric confirmation.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Warm but needs rapport; trust increases when the advisor listens, summarizes clearly, and avoids jargon.
internal_biases: loss aversion (reacts more strongly to potential downside than upside)
hidden_concerns: whether retirement timing differences between them will create pressure on cash flow
whether they have enough safeguards given they currently have no listed protection policies
